Off Calendar Apr-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PLAINTIFF ANTHONY HELD To Compel Deposition(S) Of Deft'S And Req. For Monetary Sanctions is
denied in part and granted in part. Plaintiffs may take depositions of 'persons most knowledgeable' concerning products named in the operative complaint only. The Discovery Code provides for deposition to be taken in this state as a default, and defendants have not made a sufficient showing of burden to overcome that default, accordingly the deposition may, at plaintiff's option, be conducted in California. The Court notes the exorbitant hours and billing rates expended by moving party here- rates of between $500-650/hr on a discovery motion, and a suggestion that the client may be charged $23,000 for this motion. All requests for money sanctions are denied. Prevailing party to prepare order. JUDGE CURTIS E. A. KARNOW. NOT REPORTED.
